hub_subtitle: Business English for HR leadership in international meetings

lesson.menu_title: What Is Happening Right Now — Describing Live Situations in Meetings
lesson.menu_desc: Narrating live action in meetings and operations with the present continuous -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 4 -- What Is Happening Right Now -- Describing Live Situations in Meetings
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Murielle Xavier | Aula 4 -- What Is Happening Right Now

listenings.text: Hi Murielle, it is Liam. I am calling you from the office, and it is a busy morning. Right now, the finance team is presenting the quarterly results in the big meeting room. Sofia is updating the slides while Daniel is taking notes. Two managers are reviewing the new hiring plan at the back of the room. Outside, people are moving between desks, and the phones are ringing all the time. Our main project is still ongoing, but it is on track, and we are not behind schedule. I am sending you a short update by email right now. Talk soon.

Good morning, everyone. I want to give you a quick update on what is happening this week. At the moment, we are reviewing the new onboarding process, and the team is making good progress. We are not hiring new people right now, but we are training the managers we already have. The system change is still ongoing, so a few reports are running slowly today. Please be patient. I am working closely with the operations team, and everything is on track. Thank you, and have a productive day.
